title: Rockia sandwicensis
taxonomy: Rockia sandwicensis is classified in the family Gesneriaceae. This family consists of flowering plants commonly known as the African violet family. Rockia sandwicensis is a member of the genus Rockia, which is a small genus within the Gesneriaceae family. The species is native to the Hawaiian Islands and is a type of flowering plant. Further classification details may vary depending on the specific source and taxonomic authority.
uses: Rockia sandwicensis, also known as the Ma'o hau hele or Yellow Hawaiian Hibiscus, has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for various purposes. Its flowers and leaves are rich in vitamins and minerals, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, sore throats, and skin conditions. The plant's root is also used to treat digestive issues and other internal problems. However, further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of this plant.

parts: Rockia sandwicensis, also known as Hawaiian hibiscus, is a plant with a long history of traditional use in Hawaiian medicine. The medicinal parts of this plant are the leaves, which have been used to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive issues. The leaves contain compounds such as flavonoids and saponins, which are believed to be responsible for their medicinal properties. intro: Rockia sandwicensis, also known as Hawaii hawthorn or Hawaiian hawthorn, is a plant species native to the Hawaiian Islands. The health benefits of Rockia sandwicensis include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
